mnemonics: Ricardo the Lawyer-Bot: Imagine a robot lawyer named Ricardo who reads a contract (Ricardian) and then creates a unique, verifiable digital fingerprint (hash) for it on the blockchain.
keyPoints: A Ricardian contract is a digital document that is both human-readable and machine-readable.
It is cryptographically signed and hashed, creating a unique identifier that is recorded on the blockchain.
Its primary purpose is to legally bridge the gap between the intent of a traditional contract and the performance of a smart contract.
commonMistakes: Confusing Ricardian contracts with smart contracts; a Ricardian contract defines the terms, while a smart contract executes them.
Assuming the full contract text is stored on-chain; typically, only the hash of the document is stored for verification.
